title: 10 best herbal creams for dry scalp

remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antifungal Properties: Help to combat fungal infections such as malassezia, which can contribute to dry scalp and dandruff by reducing inflammation and preventing overgrowth.
Antioxidant Properties of Herbal Extracts: Neutralize free radicals that can damage the scalp and hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing inflammation associated with dry scalp.
Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and itchiness caused by dry skin and flaking, while also helping to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with dry scalp because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in tea tree oil, such as terpinen-4-ol, inhibit the growth of fungi and yeast that can cause irritation and flaking on the scalp. By reducing inflammation and preventing fungal overgrowth, tea tree creams create an environment that promotes healthy scalp function and reduces dryness, itchiness, and flakiness, helping to alleviate dry scalp symptoms and promote a healthier scalp.
remedy_constituents: Terpinen-4-ol: This compound exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce fungal and bacterial growth on the scalp, alleviating dry scalp conditions.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): Cineole's antifungal and antiseptic properties help to soothe and calm an itchy scalp, while also reducing inflammation and promoting healthy scalp conditions.

Melaleuca Alternifolia Oil (Tri-Cyclic Terpene): This compound has potent antifungal properties, which help to control fungal infections on the scalp, such as dandruff, and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of tea tree oil, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help soothe and treat dry scalp conditions.
Stems: Utilized for their essential oils, which contain compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healthy scalp skin.
Barks: Employed for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and protect dry scalp skin.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves in 1l of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Combine 200g of the infused oil with 100g of beeswax and 50g of candelilla wax.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes until the beeswax and candelilla wax melt.
Pour the mixture into a mold or a glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemongrass cream's antimicrobial properties help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause flaking and itching on the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass cream reduce redness and swelling associated with dry scalp conditions like dandruff and psoriasis, providing relief from discomfort and irritation.

Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass cream's antioxidant properties help to protect the scalp from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature aging and hair loss.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, creams helps with dry scalp because of its exceptional soothing and moisturizing properties. The essential oils present in lemongrass creams have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help to reduce irritation and flaking on the scalp. It also helps to balance the scalp's natural pH, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties lock in moisture, keeping the scalp hydrated and comfortable, thus providing relief from dryness and itchiness.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: A terpene that helps with dry scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment, thereby alleviating itchiness and flakiness associated with dry scalp conditions.

Citrral (Cymbopogon citratus oil): A phenolic compound that possesses antifungal properties, helping to control fungal infections that can contribute to dry scalp conditions and promote a balanced scalp microbiome.
Zingiberene: A sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dry scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly limonene and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties beneficial for dry scalp treatment.
Rhyzomes: They are used as they contain active compounds like citral and geraniol that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing dryness and itchiness.
Roots: They are used because of their rich content of essential oils and nutrients that promote scalp health, reduce inflammation, and prevent fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves and 50g of beeswax for this recipe.
Infuse 200ml of jojoba oil with the dried cymbopogon citratus leaves for 2-3 weeks.
Combine 100g of shea butter and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Mix the infused jojoba oil with 20g of vitamin e oil and the combined shea butter and coconut oil mixture.
Pour the final mixture into a container and let it cool at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dry scalp conditions like dandruff and psoriasis.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents in aloe cream, such as aloin and acemannan, combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dry scalp conditions, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Moisturizing and soothing properties: Aloe vera's natural emollients and vitamins help hydrate the scalp, reducing flakiness and discomfort while also calming irritation and itchiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with dry scalp because of its natural soothing properties and rich moisturizing benefits. It contains compounds like glycoproteins and vitamins A, C, and E that help to nourish and hydrate the scalp, reducing flakiness and itchiness. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera also help to calm irritated skin and promote healthy hair growth, making it an effective treatment for dry scalp conditions. Regular use of aloe vera creams can leave the scalp feeling smooth and refreshed.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help with dry scalp by creating a soothing and protective barrier on the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation that can lead to dryness and flakiness.

Vitamins (A, C, E): Vitamins A, C, and E in Aloe vera creams help with dry scalp by promoting skin cell turnover, reducing oxidative stress, and protecting the skin from damage caused by free radicals.

Polysaccharides (Glucans): Polysaccharides like Glucans in Aloe vera creams help with dry scalp by promoting wound healing, reducing inflammation, and stimulating the growth of new skin cells to replace damaged or dead skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in gel, which provides moisturizing and soothing properties to help calm and hydrate the dry scalp.
Seeds: Contain fatty acids and vitamins that promote skin and scalp health, reducing inflammation and itchiness.
(Optional) Stems: Contain aloe-emodin, a compound that may help reduce inflammation and promote healing of the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel from mature leaves and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a bowl.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler to melt.
Combine 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil and 2 tablespoons of honey in the melted mixture.
Stir in 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and 1 teaspoon of lavender essential oil into the mixture.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian Blue Gum cream has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling associated with a dry, itchy scalp.

Antifungal and antibacterial: The cream exhibits antifungal and antibacterial activities, making it effective against fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dry scalp conditions like dandruff and seborrhoeic dermatitis.
Moisturizing and soothing: Tasmanian Blue Gum cream has moisturizing and soothing properties, which help to lock in moisture and calm irritated skin, relieving dryness, itchiness, and flakiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with dry scalp because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The camphor and eucalyptol present in Eucalyptus globulus creams help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with a dry scalp. The cooling sensation provided by the cream helps to calm the scalp, promoting relaxation and reducing flakiness. Regular application of Eucalyptus globulus creams can help to soothe and moisturize the scalp, leading to a healthy and balanced scalp.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene acts as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and itchiness associated with dry scalp conditions.

Flavonoids (Catechins and Quercetin): As antioxidants, flavonoids help protect the scalp from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting a healthy scalp environment and reducing the symptoms of dry scalp.

Tannins: These phenolic compounds possess astringent properties, which help to reduce oil production and minimize flaking, thereby providing relief from dry scalp conditions and promoting a balanced scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm a dry scalp.
Barks: Barks are used because they contain a high amount of salicylic acid, which helps to exfoliate and remove dead skin cells from the scalp, promoting healthy scalp growth.
Buds: Buds are used due to their rich content of essential oils, including eucalyptol, which provides anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to help calm and soothe a dry, itchy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and 500 grams of a base cream with a ph level between 4.5 and 5.5.
Infuse 200 milliliters of a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil with the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves for 4 to 6 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and combine it with the base cream and add 2 milliliters of vitamin e oil as an antioxidant.
Mix the ingredients well and add 5 milliliters of a natural preservative such as phenonip to extend the shelf life.
Fill the mixture into sterile containers and label them properly before storing them in a cool and dry place for up to 12 months.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help reduce redness and itching associated with a dry scalp.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Rosemary's antimicrobial agents, like rosmarinic acid, inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to dry scalp conditions like dandruff and seborrheic dermatitis.

Stimulant and expectorant properties: Rosemary's stimulant compounds, such as camphene, help increase blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and removing dead skin cells that can accumulate on a dry scalp.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, creams helps with dry scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The herb's essential oils, particularly camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin. Additionally, rosemary's ability to improve blood circulation and promote a healthy scalp environment can help to reduce flaking and itchiness associated with dry scalp conditions. This natural remedy can provide relief and promote a healthy, balanced scalp.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene helps with dry scalp by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Rosmarinic acid: As a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound, rosmarinic acid helps soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and itchiness associated with dry scalp conditions.

Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in reducing the growth of fungi and bacteria that can contribute to dry scalp conditions, promoting a balanced scalp ecosystem.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high content of antioxidants and essential oils that help soothe and calm dry scalp irritations.
Flowers: Flowers are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce irritation and promote a healthy scalp.
Stems: Stems are used for their high content of rosmarinic acid, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow to the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the leaves for 2 hours to extract the essential oil with a yield of 2 percent.
Mix 1/4 cup of distilled water with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler.
Combine the essential oil with 1/2 cup of distilled water and add it to the mixture from step 3.
Whisk the mixture for 10 minutes and pour it into a container for 30 minutes cooling time before use.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to a dry, itchy scalp by killing off pathogens and reducing inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with a dry scalp, promoting a soothing and calming effect on the skin.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the scalp's natural oils and cause dryness, promoting healthy oil production and reducing the appearance of flakiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with dry scalp because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The thymol content in thyme cream creates an environment that is unfavorable for fungal growth, thereby reducing irritation and inflammation associated with dry scalp. Additionally, thyme's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and flakiness. By promoting a healthy scalp environment, thyme creams can help alleviate dry scalp symptoms and promote a balanced scalp ecosystem. This makes it a popular natural remedy for dry scalp issues.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol, a monoterpene compound, has antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help to control the growth of fungi and bacteria that can contribute to dry scalp conditions.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol, another monoterpene compound, has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe dry, itchy scalps.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the scalp from damage, reduce inflammation, and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are used due to their antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used for their antioxidant and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation on the scalp, while also promoting a balanced scalp pH.

Barks: Although less common, thyme bark is sometimes used for its antimicrobial properties, which can help combat fungal infections and other scalp conditions that may contribute to dryness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves at peak potency in the morning for best results.
Dry the fresh leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1/4 cup of dried thymus vulgaris leaves with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours while gently stirring every 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store the herbal cream in a cool dark place.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Wormwood cream's antimicrobial properties help combat fungal and bacterial infections on the scalp, which can contribute to dryness and flakiness by preventing moisture from penetrating the skin.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in wormwood cream reduce redness, swelling, and irritation caused by dry scalp conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, or eczema, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.

Antioxidant property: Wormwood's antioxidant properties help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can accelerate aging and dryness, and also support the natural healing process of the skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium, also known as wormwood, creams helps with dry scalp because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in wormwood cream, such as thujone and camphor, soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flaking. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce redness and swelling, promoting a healthy scalp environment. By addressing the underlying causes of dry scalp, wormwood cream can help to alleviate symptoms and promote a balanced scalp ecosystem. This leads to healthier and more manageable hair.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: Thujone is a terpene that helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with dry scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Cineol (Eucalyptol): Cineol is a phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe and protect the scalp from irritation and infection, reducing dryness and flakiness.

Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate is a sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce redness, itching, and irritation associated with dry scalp, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp ecosystem.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the scalp.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing scalp irritation and promoting healthy skin.
Stems: Contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote hair growth, addressing dry scalp issues.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20 grams of artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers in a 1:2 ratio with jojoba oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids immediately.
Combine 100 grams of beeswax with 50 grams of shea butter and melt the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Add 20 grams of the artemisia absinthium infusion to the melted wax mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify completely.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold contains anti-inflammatory compounds like lutein and zeaxanthin, which help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and itchiness associated with dry scalp.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy skin and hair growth while reducing inflammation.

Moisturizing properties: Pot marigold's moisturizing properties, attributed to its rich content of glycosides and saponins, help lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy scalps, leaving them feeling soft and supple.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with dry scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream soothes and calms the scalp, reducing irritation and redness associated with dryness. The natural antibacterial compounds in Calendula help to combat scalp infections, promoting a healthy scalp environment. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness, leading to a healthier and more balanced scalp. This results in a reduced appearance of dandruff and other scalp irritations.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm an itchy, irritated dry scalp.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): As an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, Quercetin can help reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin cell growth, contributing to a reduction in dry scalp symptoms.

Carotenoids (specifically Beta-Carotene): This antioxidant can help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy environment that can help alleviate dry scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm the scalp.
Leaves: They are used for their moisturizing and emollient properties, which help to hydrate and soften the scalp.
Seeds: They are used for their oil content, which is rich in fatty acids and helps to nourish and protect the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers when they are in full bloom and dry them immediately to preserve their medicinal properties.
Combine 100 grams of dried calendula flowers with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally, to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and a small amount of vitamin e oil to create a smooth and stable cream.

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Sesame oil contains antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress on the scalp, which can contribute to dryness and irritation.
Moisturizing compounds: The cream's moisturizing compounds, such as sesamin and sesamolin, help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy scalps.
Antifungal properties: Sesame oil has antifungal properties that can help to combat fungal infections that may contribute to a dry scalp, such as dandruff or seborrheic dermatitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesamum indicum, also known as sesame, creams helps with dry scalp because of its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's nourishing and moisturizing ingredients deeply penetrate the scalp, soothing itchiness and reducing flakiness. Sesame oil's high linoleic acid content helps to lock in moisture, hydrating the scalp and promoting a healthy scalp environment. This natural remedy gently calms irritation, leaving the scalp feeling smooth and comfortable, perfect for those struggling with dry, irritated scalps. Regular use can lead to improved scalp health.
remedy_constituents: Sesamol: It helps with dry scalp by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and irritation associated with dry scalp conditions.
Sesamin: This lignan constituent helps with dry scalp by promoting blood circulation to the scalp, reducing dandruff and flaking associated with dry scalp conditions.
Fatty acids (mainly oleic acid): These help with dry scalp by providing moisturizing and emollient properties, soothing and hydrating the scalp to reduce dryness and itchiness.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, which help to soothe and moisturize the scalp, reducing dryness and itchiness.
Leaves: The leaves contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm and soothe the scalp, reducing irritation and promoting healthy hair growth.
Seeds: Sesame seeds also contain natural humectants that help to lock in moisture, leaving the scalp feeling soft and hydrated.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried sesamum indicum flowers and leave them to steep in 500ml of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids retaining the oil for further processing.
Mix 50g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter with 100ml of the infused oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax and shea butter have melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and solidify before scooping into containers for use.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol helps to relieve itchiness, inflammation, and discomfort associated with a dry scalp by providing a cooling sensation that calms the skin and reduces irritation.
Eucalyptus Oil: Eucalyptus oil has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling on the scalp, while also promoting healthy blood flow and circulation.

Wintergreen Extract (Methyl Salicylate): Wintergreen extract contains methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever that helps to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and flaking associated with dryness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens, also known as wintergreen, creams helps with dry scalp because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream's main active ingredient, methyl salicylate, helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing flakiness and itchiness associated with dry scalp. Additionally, Gaultheria procumbens contains salicylic acid, which gently exfoliates the scalp, removing dead skin cells and promoting a healthy scalp environment. This natural combination helps to alleviate dry scalp symptoms, promoting a smooth and comfortable scalp.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: It helps with dry scalp by reducing inflammation and pain due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe an irritated scalp.
Methyl salicylate: It helps with dry scalp by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling, and promoting blood flow to the scalp, which can help to alleviate dryness and itchiness.

Gaultherin: It helps with dry scalp by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect the scalp from damage, reduce inflammation, and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their rich content of tannins, which help to soothe and protect the scalp.
Roots: The roots are often used because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce irritation and promote healing on the scalp.
Barks: The barks are sometimes used as they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that can help to moisturize and calm the scalp, reducing dryness and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in mid-summer when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested gaultheria procumbens in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried gaultheria procumbens in 8 ounces of jojoba oil for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids in 10 minutes.
Whip 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter for 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with dry scalp because of its unique combination of soothing and nourishing properties. The lavender oil in these creams has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm irritation and reduce flaking on the scalp. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing ingredients work to hydrate and lock in moisture, leaving the scalp feeling soft and supple. As a result, dry scalp symptoms such as itchiness and redness are significantly reduced, promoting a healthy and comfortable scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which help to soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and itchiness associated with dry scalp.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: English lavender contains antifungal and antibacterial agents that inhibit the growth of microorganisms that can contribute to dry scalp conditions, such as dandruff, psoriasis, and eczema.

Moisturizing and emollient effects: Lavender creams often contain moisturizing and emollients like vitamin E and jojoba oil that help to lock in moisture, soothe dryness, and improve the overall condition of the scalp, reducing flaking and itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are good for soothing and calming the scalp.
Seeds: Contains a high concentration of fatty acids, which help to moisturize and nourish the scalp, relieving dryness and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in sweet almond oil for 14 days to create a potent extract.
Combine 20 grams of the infused oil with 80 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 160°f.
Remove the mixture from heat and allow it to cool and thicken at room temperature for 1 hour before whipping with a hand mixer for 5 minutes.
Transfer the whipped cream to an air-tight container, label and date it, and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or until used.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as kaempferol and quercetin, help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can alleviate itching and flaking associated with dry scalp.

Antimicrobial properties: The herb's antimicrobial compounds, like triterpenoids and flavonoids, inhibit the growth of fungi, bacteria, and yeast that can contribute to dry scalp conditions, promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Soothing and moisturizing properties: Purple coneflower cream's emollients and humectants, such as glycerin and fatty acids, help lock in moisture and soothe dryness, reducing irritation and discomfort on the scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain a rich source of saponins and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help soothe and calm dry scalps.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the purple coneflower contain bioactive compounds like phenolic acids and flavonoids, which exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities that can effectively combat fungal infections causing dry scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality echinacea purpurea roots and dried them according to proper procedures.
Combine 20g of dried echinacea purpurea roots with 100ml of distilled water in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes, allowing the herbal extract to steep and infuse.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another heat-resistant container, discarding solids and reserving the liquid extract.
Mix the echinacea purpurea extract with 200g of petroleum jelly and 10ml of coconut oil, whipping until a smooth and creamy consistency is achieved.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with dry scalp because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's rich, hydrating properties deeply moisturize the scalp, reducing flakiness and itchiness associated with dryness. Additionally, purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness and swelling, promoting a healthy scalp environment. By providing long-lasting hydration and soothing relief, these creams can help alleviate dry scalp symptoms and promote a comfortable, manageable scalp condition.

Stinging nettle creams helps with dry scalp because of its unique ability to moisturize and soothe irritated skin. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and itching, while its natural astringent qualities help to balance the scalp's natural oil production, reducing flakiness and dandruff. Additionally, stinging nettle's rich antioxidants and vitamins A, C, and E nourish the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and a vibrant, shiny coat. By providing long-lasting relief from dryness and itchiness, stinging nettle creams offer a natural solution for dry scalps.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle contains compounds like caffeic acid and quercetin that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe an itchy and irritated scalp.
Antioxidant properties: Nettle's rich antioxidant content helps protect the scalp from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dryness.

Immunomodulatory properties: Nettle has been shown to modulate the immune response, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of conditions like dandruff and psoriasis that often accompany a dry scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, stinging nettle roots can help soothe and calm irritated scalps while promoting healthy hair growth.
Seeds: Abundant in fatty acids and vitamins, stinging nettle seeds add moisturizing and nourishing properties to creams, helping to lock in moisture and hydrate the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves and dry them using air circulation or shade drying for 2-3 days.
Combine 100 grams dried nettle leaves with 500 milliliters boiling water and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a heat-proof container and discard solids.
Mix 20 percent of the strained liquid with a neutral cream base such as coconut oil and beeswax and heat to 70-80 degrees celsius.
Pour the hot cream mixture into sterilized jars and seal tightly, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with dry scalp because they contain antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that soothe and nourish the scalp. The anti-inflammatory properties in dandelion help to reduce redness and irritation, while its antifungal and antibacterial agents combat scalp infections. Additionally, dandelion's moisturizing effects help to lock in moisture and hydrate the scalp, relieving dryness and itchiness. This natural and gentle approach can bring relief from flaky, itchy scalps and promote a healthy, balanced scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, reduce redness and swelling associated with dry scalp conditions like psoriasis and eczema.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage the scalp and worsen dryness, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing oxidative stress.

Soothing and moisturizing properties: The cream's emollient and humectant properties, derived from dandelion's sap and leaves, provide long-lasting moisture and soothing relief for itchy, flaky scalps, helping to restore the scalp's natural barrier function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, helping to soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Fruits: High in vitamins and minerals, including potassium and magnesium, which can help to nourish and hydrate the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Gather and prepare the dried taraxacum officinale leaves by rinsing them gently in cold water.
Combine 20g of dried leaves with 100ml of hot distilled water allowing for a 10-minute infusion period.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Mix the infused liquid with 50g of beeswax and 30g of coconut oil in a double boiler stirring until melted and smooth.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for approximately 1 hour before whipping and storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with dry scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as boswellic acids, work to soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and flaking. Additionally, frankincense's natural astringent qualities help to balance the skin's natural pH, purifying the scalp and unclogging pores. This results in a healthy, moisturized scalp that is less prone to dryness, itchiness, and irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as boswellic acid, help to reduce redness and swelling associated with a dry scalp, promoting a healthy and calm environment for hair growth.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in frankincense creams, including beta-carotene and flavonoids, work to neutralize free radicals that can damage the scalp and hair, helping to prevent further irritation and dryness.

Anti-microbial and antifungal properties: Frankincense's antimicrobial and antifungal compounds help to combat infections and irritations caused by fungi, bacteria, and other microorganisms, reducing symptoms of dandruff, itchiness, and flakiness.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the underground stems that store nutrients and water, making them rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm dry scalp irritation.
Barks: Frankincense barks contain a high concentration of terpenes, which provide natural antiseptic and anti-itch properties to help alleviate dry scalp symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Crush 2 tablespoons of dried boswellia serrata resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Heat 4 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler until it reaches a temperature of 120°f.
Add the crushed boswellia serrata powder to the melted coconut oil and stir until well combined.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes before transferring it to an empty cream jar.
Store the herbal cream in a cool dark place and use within 6 weeks of preparation or refrigerate for up to 3 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ironwort creams helps with dry scalp because of its unique properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's anti-inflammatory agents work to reduce redness and itching, while its moisturizing ingredients help to lock in moisture and hydrate the scalp. As a result, ironwort creams can help to alleviate flaking, itchiness, and irritation associated with dry scalp, leaving the scalp feeling soft, smooth, and manageable.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ironwort's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and reducing irritation caused by dryness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ironwort's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and itching associated with dry skin conditions like dandruff and psoriasis.

Antimicrobial properties: Ironwort's antimicrobial agents, including volatile oils and alkaloids, help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can exacerbate dry scalp conditions, such as eczema, ringworm, and folliculitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of flavonoids and saponins, which help to soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin.
Barks: The barks have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and itching associated with dry scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sideritis scardica leaves and rinse them with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the sideritis scardica leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 8 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Mix 30g of dried sideritis scardica leaves with 150ml of olive oil and stir the mixture well to create a potent infusion.
Heat the infusion mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 1 hour, then strain it through a cheesecloth to extract the herbal essence.
Add 10g of beeswax to the extracted essence and heat the mixture in a microwave-safe bowl on high for 30 seconds to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with dry scalp because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's active ingredients, including hyperforin and hypericin, work to reduce redness and flaking, promoting a healthy scalp environment. Additionally, St John's Wort is known for its antimicrobial effects, helping to combat fungal infections that can contribute to dryness and itchiness. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal St John's Wort creams provide long-lasting relief from dry scalp symptoms.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This active compound has anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative effects, which can help reduce redness and itching associated with a dry scalp.
Hypotensive activity: St. John's Wort creams may help improve blood circulation to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dandruff-related flakiness.
Antimicrobial properties: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort have been shown to inhibit the growth of Malassezia fungi, which can contribute to dry scalp conditions like seborrheic dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing and calming dry scalps.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide antioxidant and antimicrobial activities that can help combat fungal infections and reduce inflammation on the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers at peak potency and quality.
Combine the harvested herbs with 200ml of olive oil in a glass jar, ensuring the herbs are fully submerged.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow for maceration and extraction.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding solids and retaining liquid.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 100ml of the infused oil, heating gently in a double boiler until melted and blended.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with dry scalp because of its remarkable soothing and calming properties. The natural anti-inflammatory compounds present in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, work wonders in reducing irritation and redness caused by dry scalp conditions like dandruff and psoriasis. Additionally, chamomile's emollient properties help to moisturize and nourish the scalp, leaving it feeling soft, supple, and refreshed. This natural remedy provides long-lasting relief from itchiness and flakiness, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dry scalp conditions.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dryness and irritation, promoting a healthier scalp environment.

Soothing and moisturizing properties: The soothing and moisturizing compounds in chamomile, such as essential fatty acids and glycerides, help to lock in moisture and calm dry, itchy scalps, leaving them feeling soft and manageable.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile roots help soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Flowers: With their calming properties and gentle astringent effect, chamomile flowers provide relief from dryness and itchiness of the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them in shade or air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Grind dried flowers into fine powder using mortar and pestle and sift through 100-mesh sieve.
Combine 20% matricaria chamomilla powder with 80% sweet almond oil and beeswax in double boiler.
Heat mixture to 160°f over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until wax melts and emulsifies.
Remove from heat, let cool slightly, then pour into sterilized containers and refrigerate for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort creams helps with dry scalp because they harness the natural anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties of the herb. The cream's soothing and moisturizing agents help to calm irritated skin, reducing redness and flaking associated with dry scalp conditions. Additionally, the antioxidant-rich properties of pennywort help to repair damaged skin cells, promoting healthy hair growth and a balanced scalp environment. As a result, Asiatic pennywort creams provide effective relief from dry, itchy, and flaky scalps, leaving users with a healthy and manageable mane.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Asiatic pennywort creams contain antioxidants that help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dryness and irritation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Asiatic pennywort creams reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with dry scalp conditions, providing relief and soothing comfort to the scalp.
Moisturizing properties: The cream's moisturizing properties help lock in moisture, hydrating the scalp and hair follicles, which can help alleviate dryness, itchiness, and flakiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Rhyzomes: Contain compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and itching associated with dry scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried centella asiatica leaves from a reputable supplier weighing approximately 100 grams.
Combine the dried leaves with 200ml of organic coconut oil in a double boiler for a minimum of 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix 20% of the infused oil with 80% of cooled down distilled water and add 2% of liquid glycerin as an emulsifier.
Whip the mixture in a stainless steel bowl until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency taking approximately 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog holly creams helps with dry scalp because they contain natural ingredients that moisturize and soothe the skin. The antifungal and antibacterial properties of herbs like tea tree oil, neem, and lavender work together to reduce inflammation and eliminate flakes, leaving the scalp feeling calm and refreshed. Additionally, dog holly creams often include emollients such as coconut oil and shea butter that deeply hydrate the skin, providing long-lasting relief from dryness and itchiness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Dog holly cream has anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which helps to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with a dry scalp, providing relief from discomfort.
Antifungal property: The herb's antifungal properties help to combat fungal infections that can contribute to dandruff, itchiness, and flakiness on the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Emollient and moisturizing property: Dog holly cream is rich in emollients like omega-3 fatty acids, which deeply nourish and moisturize the scalp, helping to lock in moisture and reduce dryness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Rhyzomes: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antioxidant and antibacterial activities, beneficial for reducing scalp inflammation and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ruscus aculeatus leaves in summer months.
Dry the fresh leaves using air circulation or oven drying at low temperature.
Grind 20g of dried ruscus aculeatus leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix 10g of the powdered herb with 50ml of coconut oil in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth to remove solids, then store the cream at room temperature.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for dry scalp are topical treatments containing natural ingredients that help soothe and moisturize the scalp, reducing itchiness and flakiness. These creams are effective in addressing dry scalp issues due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The best herbal creams for dry scalp include Melaleuca alternifolia, Aloe barbadensis, Rosmarinus officinalis, Urtica dioica, Cymbopogon citratus, Calendula officinalis, Sesamum indicum, Cinnamomum camphora, Ginkgo biloba, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, dry scalp can lead to hair loss, discomfort, and embarrassment, significantly impacting one's self-confidence and quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry scalp that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and itching associated with these conditions. Additionally, herbal creams may be used to treat skin conditions such as acne, rosacea, and seborrheic keratoses, offering natural alternatives to traditional treatments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with dry scalp is a blend of tea tree oil, aloe vera, and neem. Tea tree oil has antifungal properties that combat dandruff and itchiness. Aloe vera soothes and hydrates the scalp, reducing inflammation and flakiness. Neem, rich in antioxidants, helps to regulate the scalp's natural oil production and prevent further dryness. Applying a mixture of these three herbal creams can provide long-lasting relief and promote a healthy scalp. Regular use can also prevent future dry scalp issues.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Current pharmaceutical design, herbal creams are good for dry scalp because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that improve local metabolism when applied externally. The study's comprehensive search of multiple databases, including Scopus, PubMed, and Google Scholar, found that many medicinal plants and natural compounds, such as Urtica dioica, Humulus lupulus, Serenoa repens, Vitis vinifera, Pygeum africanum, Cucurbita pepo, etc., have shown potential in reducing hair loss. The study's results suggest that herbal extracts and formulations derived from plants may be effective in reducing hair loss directly or indirectly, implying that they can also alleviate dry scalp conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with dry scalp because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea tree oil present in these creams reduces the growth of fungi and bacteria, which can cause irritation and itching on the scalp. It also helps to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and inflammation. Additionally, tea tree oil has antifungal properties, which can help to eliminate the root cause of dry scalp conditions such as dandruff and psoriasis, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritation, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Limonene: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, reducing the growth of microorganisms that can exacerbate dry scalp conditions.
Caryophyllene: Displays anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which can help to control fungal infections and soothe dry, itchy scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most used part due to their high concentration of tea tree oil, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help soothe and treat dry scalp conditions.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia contain a smaller amount of tea tree oil, but it's still used in some products due to its potential moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Tea tree buds are also used, particularly for their ability to reduce inflammation and promote a healthy scalp environment, which can help alleviate dry scalp symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves in 1l of boiling distilled water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discard the solids.
Mix 250g of beeswax and 250g of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Add 100g of the infused melaleuca alternifolia liquid and 20g of vitamin e oil to the melted wax mixture.
Pour the mixture into a prepared cream jar and let it cool and solidify for 30-40 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with dry scalp because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as fatty acids and amino acids, which work together to soothe and calm the scalp. It helps to reduce irritation, itching, and flaking, promoting a healthy scalp and encouraging hair growth. The natural cooling effect also helps to reduce stress and tension on the scalp, leading to a more balanced and hydrated scalp.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Helps with dry scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting the growth of healthy skin cells.
Aloe-emodin: Helps with dry scalp by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated scalp skin.
Saponins: Helps with dry scalp by reducing dandruff and flaking, thanks to their surfactant properties that help remove dead skin cells and other debris.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of aloe vera gel, which is rich in soothing and moisturizing properties that help to relieve dry scalp symptoms.
Seeds: They are used for their oil-rich content, which is often extracted and added to creams to provide nourishing and protective benefits for the scalp.
Roots: They are used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds that can help to soothe and calm an itchy or irritated scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 pure aloe barbadensis leaves from mature plants for optimal gel content.
Extract the aloe gel by cutting off the leaf tips and scooping out the clear inner pulp.
Mix 100g of the extracted aloe gel with 50g of distilled water and 25g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture for 5 minutes as it melts the beeswax and combines with the aloe gel and water.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool before transferring it to sterilized containers for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams helps with dry scalp because of its soothing and nourishing properties. The active compounds in rosemary, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, have anti-inflammatory and antifungal effects that calm irritation and reduce flaking. Additionally, rosemary's rich antioxidant content helps to protect the scalp from environmental stressors and promote healthy hair growth. By applying rosemary cream to the scalp, individuals can experience relief from dryness and itchiness, leading to a healthier and more balanced scalp.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: It helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and reducing dryness.
Rosmarinic acid: It acts as an antioxidant, protecting the scalp from damage caused by free radicals and promoting a balanced scalp environment, which helps to reduce dryness and itchiness.
Cineole: It has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and combat fungal or bacterial infections that can contribute to dry scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain essential oils that help to soothe and calm dry scalp irritation.
Leaves: They have antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help to reduce flaking and itching.
Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants that can help to protect the scalp from damage and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 cups of rosmarinus officinalis leaves in 4 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids then mix in 1 cup of beeswax.
Heat the mixture to 180-190 degrees fahrenheit and hold for 5 minutes.
Cool the mixture to 120-130 degrees fahrenheit then whip with 1/2 cup of shea butter.
Pour the final mixture into containers and let it cool and solidify completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams helps with dry scalp because they contain uric acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream also contains antioxidants that help to reduce flakiness and promote a healthy scalp. The herb is rich in vitamins and minerals that nourish the scalp, reducing dryness and itchiness. By providing long-lasting hydration and protecting the scalp from environmental stressors, Urtica dioica creams can help to alleviate dry scalp symptoms and promote a healthy, flake-free scalp.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: They help with dry scalp by reducing inflammation and itching due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Flavonoids: They aid in soothing and calming the scalp, reducing irritation and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Saponins: They help by creating a cleansing effect on the scalp, removing dead skin cells and promoting the growth of healthy hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of urtica extracts, which can help soothe and calm dry scalp irritation.
Stems: Employed for their anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and itching associated with dry scalp conditions.
Roots: Utilized for their antioxidant and antifungal properties, which can promote a healthy scalp environment and reduce dryness and flakiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pint of fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their highest moisture content.
Chop the fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers into small pieces and allow them to wilt for 24 hours.
Steep 1 cup of the wilted urtica dioica leaves and flowers in 2 cups of water for 10 minutes to make a liquid infusion.
Strain the liquid infusion using cheesecloth and then mix it with 1 cup of petroleum jelly or beeswax to create a thick paste.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160°f for 20 minutes to create a smooth and even herbal urtica dioica cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams helps with dry scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in lemongrass oil, found in Cymbopogon citratus, help to reduce redness and irritation associated with dry scalp conditions. The oil's antifungal properties also help to combat dandruff and other scalp infections, promoting a healthy scalp environment. Regular use of these creams can lead to improved scalp health, reduced flaking, and a more balanced scalp pH. This results in a smoother, more comfortable scalp.
remedy_constituents: Citral: Helps with dry scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting the growth of new hair.
Geranial: Helps with dry scalp by exhibiting antimicrobial properties that prevent fungal and bacterial infections that can cause irritation and flaking.
Limonene: Helps with dry scalp by reducing dandruff and promoting a healthy scalp environment through its antifungal and astringent properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in citronella oil, which has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and moisturize the scalp.
Leaves: They also contain other essential oils like limonene and beta-pinene, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to reduce irritation and promote scalp healing.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes to create a strong herbal infusion.
Strain the infusion and discard the solids then let it cool to room temperature.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of the cooled herbal infusion to the melted wax mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a mold and let it set for 30 minutes to an hour before removing and packaging.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with dry scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The plant's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, work to reduce irritation and redness on the scalp. Calendula's ability to promote healthy skin cell growth and regeneration can also help to repair and moisturize dry, flaky skin. As a result, the cream can provide long-lasting relief from dry scalp conditions, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment. This makes it an effective natural remedy for soothing dry scalp.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce irritation and inflammation associated with dry scalp.

Phenolic acids: These compounds possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, reduce oxidative stress, and prevent infections that may contribute to dry scalp.

Nordamnacanthal: This compound has been shown to have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help control fungal infections and reduce inflammation that may exacerbate dry scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties help soothe and protect the scalp.
Leaves: They contain antioxidants and fatty acids that promote skin health and reduce inflammation on the scalp.
Seeds: They are rich in essential fatty acids, which can help moisturize and nourish the scalp, alleviating dryness and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency and full of essential oils.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low temperature oven at 100°f for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Steep 2 ounces of dried calendula flowers in 4 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Mix 1/2 cup of distilled water with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Blend 2 ounces of the calendula infusion with the mixture from step 4 and whip until it thickens and emulsifies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sesamum indicum creams helps with dry scalp because of its rich emollient and moisturizing properties. The cream is derived from the seeds of the Sesame plant, which is known for its nourishing and soothing effects on the skin. The fatty acids and antioxidants present in the cream help to deeply moisturize the scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness. Additionally, Sesamum indicum creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm irritated skin and promote a healthy scalp environment, leading to a reduction in dry scalp symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Sesamin: It helps to reduce inflammation and promote skin health, which can alleviate dry scalp symptoms.
Sesamolin: It has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and protect the scalp from dryness and irritation.
Fatty Acids (particularly Oleic and Linoleic Acids): They help to moisturize and nourish the scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used due to their high content of essential fatty acids, particularly sesamin, which has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that help soothe and hydrate the scalp.
Leaves: Utilized for their sesamin and sesamolin content, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote skin health, providing relief from dry scalp.
Fruits: Employed for their moisturizing and emollient properties, helping to lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50g of sesamum indicum seeds, 100g of coconut oil and 50g of beeswax, and sterilize equipment.
Combine 50g of sesamum indicum seeds with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler, and heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, obtaining a liquid mixture.
Add 50g of beeswax to the liquid mixture in the double boiler, and heat for another 20 minutes.
Remove from heat, let cool, and whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it becomes creamy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum camphora creams helps with dry scalp because they possess anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and redness. The camphor in the cream helps to stimulate blood flow, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing flaking. Additionally, Cinnamomum camphora creams have a cooling effect, which can provide instant relief from itchiness and discomfort associated with dry scalp, making it an effective natural remedy for scalp issues. Regular use can lead to a healthier scalp and manageable hair.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce redness and irritation associated with dry scalp conditions.
Eugenol: This phenolic compound has antifungal properties, inhibiting the growth of fungal infections that can contribute to dry scalp conditions.
Bornyl acetate: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and flakiness associated with dry scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of cinnamaldehyde, a compound that helps to soothe and calm the scalp.
Barks: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which can help to reduce irritation and promote healthy scalp conditions.
Stems: Employed for their camphor content, which has a cooling effect and can help to relieve dryness and itchiness on the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Melt 2 tablespoons of cocoa butter in a double boiler over low heat for 5 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of cinnamomum camphora essential oil to the melted cocoa butter.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of beeswax into the mixture and let it melt for 2 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a heat-resistant glass jar and let it cool to room temperature for 30 minutes.
Store the herbal cinnamomum camphora cream in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with dry scalp because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredients, derived from the leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree, work to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flaking. As a result, the cream promotes a healthy scalp environment, allowing natural oils to balance and moisturize the skin. This leads to a reduction in dryness and itchiness, leaving the scalp feeling smooth and comfortable. Regular use can also promote a healthy hair growth.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritation on the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.
Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): Exert antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce redness, itchiness, and flakiness associated with dry scalp.
Ginkgotoxin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to calm inflammation and irritation on the scalp, ultimately leading to a reduction in dryness and itchiness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their medicinal properties, Ginkgo biloba leaves are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to soothe and moisturize the scalp.

Seeds: The seeds of the Ginkgo biloba tree have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce irritation and promote healthy scalp skin.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow to the scalp, making it a popular ingredient in creams for dry scalp.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 50 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine 100 grams of sweet almond oil and 50 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the oil and shea butter mixture to 60-70 degrees celsius and stir for 10 minutes.
Add the ground ginkgo biloba powder to the oil mixture and stir for an additional 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and let it cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with dry scalp because of its soothing and calming properties. The essential oils present in the cream, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help to reduce irritation and flaking on the scalp. The cream also has moisturizing properties that help to lock in moisture and soothe dry skin, promoting a healthy scalp environment. Regular use can lead to a reduction in flaking and an improvement in scalp health.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as an antifungal agent, helping to control the growth of fungi that can contribute to dry scalp conditions.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and soothing irritation associated with dry scalp.
Caryophyllene: Possesses antibacterial properties, helping to eliminate bacterial overgrowth that can lead to dry scalp and flakiness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the scalp.
Flowers: Known for their calming and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote a healthy scalp.
Essential oil extracted from Flowers: Noted for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, and promote a healthy environment for hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with clean scissors to avoid contamination.
Combine the dried flowers with 2 tablespoons of oil in a saucepan and heat it gently for 10 minutes at 100-150 degrees fahrenheit.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it steep for 1 hour to allow the flowers to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to separate the oil from the flowers.
Mix the infused oil with 1 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of shea butter in a double boiler and heat it until melted and smooth.
